lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. How are you?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: I've been better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: You may or may not be aware, but a plague has spread across West Ardougne. Now, so far, our efforts to contain it have been largely successful, for the most part.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: However, some sheep recently escaped from a farm near the city. When they were found, we noticed that they were strangely discoloured, so we asked the mourners to examine them.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: They believe that the sheep have become infected with the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure it's the plague?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Well the mourners seemed convinced and they're the experts. Even if it's not though, the sheep are clearly diseased in some way.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: As the councillor responsible for public health and safety here in East Ardougne, I am in charge of dealing with this problem.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: We need someone to herd these sheep into a safe enclosure, kill them quickly and cleanly and then dispose of the remains hygienically in a special incinerator.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Unfortunately, nobody wants to risk catching the plague, and I am unable to find someone willing to undertake this mission for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can do that for you.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: You will? That is excellent news! Head to the enclosure we have set up on Farmer Brumty's land to the north of the city; the four infected sheep should still be somewhere in that vicinity.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Before you will be allowed to enter the enclosure, however, you must ensure you have some kind of protective clothing to prevent contagion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find some protective clothing then?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Doctor Orbon should have something suitable. You should be able to find him in the chapel just north of here.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Please also take this poisoned sheep feed; we believe poisoning the sheep will minimise the risk of airborne contamination, and is of course also more humane to the sheep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will I know which sheep are infected?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: The poor creatures have developed strangely discoloured wool and flesh. You should have no trouble spotting them. There should be four in total.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not a job for me.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: I do not blame you adventurer. It is not particularly pleasant work. My problem remains however. If you know of anyone suitable please send them this way; time is of the essence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's life for you.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: That's what concerns me... There might not be much life around here soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Hello, is there any news about how our sheep keep getting infected with the plague?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not that I know of, sorry.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: I'd sure like to know why we can't contain it. Please raise the issue next time you see the Head Mourner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep it in mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again sir.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Well hello again adventurer! How are you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thank you. And yourself?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: I'm okay. Sadly some more diseased sheep have appeared so I have them to deal with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you need me to help again?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Thank you for the kind offer. You've done enough though. We'll find someone else to deal with these ones.

sections.title: Standard dialogue after Song of the Elves

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Well hello again adventurer! How are you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thank you. And yourself?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Not too bad, I've a lot to do though. King Lathas left plenty of things for us to clean up.
